## 📅 Daily Plans

### Day 1: Historic Old Delhi and Street Food
Dive into the heart of Delhi as you explore the colossal Red Fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that showcases the grandeur of Mughal architecture. Afterward, visit the serene Jama Masjid, one of the largest mosques in India. Don’t miss a thrilling rickshaw ride through the bustling lanes of Chandni Chowk, where you can indulge in delectable street food like crispy paranthas and sweet jalebis. The blend of history and culinary delights will surely leave you enchanted.

### Day 2: New Delhi Heritage Landmarks
On your second day, immerse yourself in the elegance of New Delhi. Begin with a visit to the stunning Humayun's Tomb, a magnificent example of Mughal architecture. Next, drive past the iconic India Gate and the impressive Rashtrapati Bhavan, the official residence of the President of India. Cap off your day by exploring the ancient Qutub Minar, the tallest brick minaret in the world, which stands as a testament to India's rich history.

### Day 3: Lotus Temple & Departure
Spend your final morning in Delhi at the tranquil Lotus Temple, known for its unique flower-like architecture and peaceful ambiance. Afterward, head to Dilli Haat, an open-air food and craft bazaar, where you can browse through contemporary crafts and enjoy a variety of cuisines. This delightful experience will be the perfect way to wrap up your trip before heading to the airport or station.
